comment: CA-allowlist TLS pinning for model-download egress. Both platforms restrict download-host trust to these root CAs (macOS: SPKI match in the URLSession challenge handler for matching hosts; Windows: reqwest built-in roots disabled, these PEMs installed as the only trust anchors on the download client). This is defense-in-depth — per-artifact SHA256 in shared/models/manifest.json is the primary integrity control. Pin level is the ROOT CA (with cross-family backups), not leaf/intermediate: leaves rotate ~90 days and HF/GitHub move between CDNs; roots are stable for decades. Captured live 2026-06-09 (huggingface.co + cas-bridge.xethub.hf.co → Amazon Root CA 1; github.com → USERTrust ECC; release-assets.githubusercontent.com → ISRG Root X1; developer.download.nvidia.com → DigiCert Global Root G3). Escape hatch: FILEID_DISABLE_TLS_PINNING=1 reverts to system roots (logged loudly; changes validation only, never adds egress). Rotation: re-run shared/scripts/check_tls_pins.sh --capture before each release; if a host moved to a CA outside this set, add its root + ship an update.
